They feared exceedingly and said, 'Who can this be, that even the wind and the sea obey Him!'

history_records.text: The Sea of Galilee is known for sudden violent storms due to its geography

question: What was the feeding of the four thousand?

scripture_says.text: They had seven loaves and a few little fish; Jesus took them, gave thanks, and broke them
They all ate and were filled; they took up seven large baskets of fragments; those who ate were four thousand men
Jesus later reminded the disciples of both feedings to teach them about faith

history_records.text: This miracle is distinct from the feeding of the 5,000; both Matthew and Mark record both events

question: What was the cursing of the fig tree?

scripture_says.text: Seeing a fig tree with leaves but no fruit, Jesus said, 'Let no one eat fruit from you ever again'
In the morning they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots; Peter said, 'The fig tree has withered'
Jesus used the occasion to teach about faith and prayer

question: What was the coin in the fish's mouth?

scripture_says.text: Those who received the temple tax asked Peter if Jesus paid it; Peter said yes
Jesus said, 'Go to the sea, cast in a hook; take the fish that comes up first; in its mouth you will find a piece of money; take that and give it to them for Me and you'

history_records.text: The stater (coin) was worth four drachmas, enough for two people's temple tax

question: What was the miraculous catch of fish?

scripture_says.text: Jesus told Simon to launch out into the deep and let down the nets; Simon had fished all night and caught nothing
They caught so many fish the nets were breaking; they filled both boats until they began to sink
Peter fell at Jesus' knees saying, 'Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord!'
After the resurrection, a similar catch occurred with 153 large fish

question: What was healing the royal official's son?

scripture_says.text: A nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum begged Jesus to come down and heal him
Jesus said, 'Go your way; your son lives.' The man believed and went his way
The fever left him at the seventh hour, the same hour Jesus spoke; the man and his whole household believed
Peter's mother-in-law lay sick with a fever; they told Jesus about her; He took her hand and lifted her up
He touched her hand and the fever left her; she arose and served them

history_records.text: This occurred in Capernaum, Jesus' base of ministry in Galilee

question: What was healing the man with the withered hand?

scripture_says.text: A man with a withered hand was in the synagogue; Jesus told him to step forward
Jesus asked, 'Is it lawful to do good on the Sabbath?' He told the man to stretch out his hand; it was restored
The Pharisees plotted against Him because He healed on the Sabbath

question: What was healing the centurion's servant?

scripture_says.text: A centurion came saying, 'Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, dreadfully tormented'
The centurion said, 'I am not worthy that You should come; only speak a word and my servant will be healed'
Jesus marveled and said, 'I have not found such great faith, not even in Israel!'
Jesus said, 'Go; as you have believed, so let it be done.' The servant was healed that same hour

question: What was raising the widow's son at Nain?

scripture_says.text: A dead man was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow; the Lord had compassion on her
He touched the open coffin and said, 'Young man, I say to you, arise.' He sat up and began to speak
Fear came upon all, and they glorified God, saying, 'A great prophet has risen among us'

history_records.text: Nain is a village in lower Galilee, identified with modern Nein

scripture_says.text: Jairus, a ruler of the synagogue, begged Jesus saying, 'My little daughter lies at the point of death'
While He was speaking, word came, 'Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the Teacher further?'
Jesus said, 'The child is not dead, but sleeping.' He took her hand and said, 'Little girl, arise.' She arose and walked

question: What was healing the woman with the issue of blood?

scripture_says.text: A woman had a flow of blood for twelve years; she had spent all she had and was no better
She came behind Him in the crowd and touched His garment; immediately she was healed
Jesus said, 'Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be healed of your affliction'

history_records.text: Under Mosaic law, her condition made her ceremonially unclean (Lev.15.25)

question: What was healing two blind men?

scripture_says.text: Two blind men followed Him, crying out, 'Son of David, have mercy on us!'
Jesus asked, 'Do you believe that I am able to do this?' They said, 'Yes, Lord.' He touched their eyes saying, 'According to your faith let it be to you'
Their eyes were opened

question: What was healing the mute demoniac?

scripture_says.text: A man, mute and demon-possessed, was brought to Jesus; when the demon was cast out, the mute spoke
The multitudes marveled, saying, 'It was never seen like this in Israel!'
The Pharisees said, 'He casts out demons by the ruler of the demons'

question: What was healing the paralytic lowered through the roof?

scripture_says.text: Four men brought a paralytic; unable to reach Jesus because of the crowd, they uncovered the roof and lowered him down
When Jesus saw their faith, He said, 'Son, your sins are forgiven you'
Jesus asked which was easier to say, then told the man to take up his bed and walk
He arose, took up his bed, and went out in the presence of them all

question: What was healing at the pool of Bethesda?

scripture_says.text: At the pool called Bethesda lay a multitude of sick people; a man had been ill thirty-eight years
Jesus asked, 'Do you want to be made well?' He told him, 'Rise, take up your bed and walk'
Immediately the man was made well, took up his bed, and walked. That day was the Sabbath

history_records.text: Archaeological excavations have uncovered a pool with five porticos near St. Anne's Church in Jerusalem

question: What was healing the man born blind?

scripture_says.text: The disciples asked whose sin caused the man to be born blind; Jesus said it was so God's works would be revealed
Jesus made clay with His saliva, anointed the man's eyes, and told him to wash in the pool of Siloam; he came back seeing
The man said, 'One thing I know: that though I was blind, now I see'
Jesus revealed Himself as the Son of Man; the man worshiped Him

history_records.text: The Pool of Siloam has been excavated in the City of David area of Jerusalem

question: What was healing the crippled woman?

scripture_says.text: A woman had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years; she was bent over and could not straighten herself
Jesus said, 'Woman, you are loosed from your infirmity.' He laid His hands on her; she was made straight and glorified God
The ruler of the synagogue was indignant because Jesus healed on the Sabbath
Jesus called her 'a daughter of Abraham whom Satan has bound for eighteen years'

question: What was healing the man with dropsy?

scripture_says.text: Jesus went into the house of a ruler of the Pharisees on the Sabbath; a man with dropsy was there
Jesus asked, 'Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?' They kept silent; He healed him and let him go
Jesus asked which of them, if their donkey fell into a pit, would not pull it out on the Sabbath; they could not answer

history_records.text: Dropsy (edema) is abnormal swelling due to fluid retention

question: What was the cleansing of the ten lepers?

scripture_says.text: Ten men who were lepers stood afar off and cried, 'Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!'
He said, 'Go, show yourselves to the priests.' As they went, they were cleansed
One of them returned to give thanks, falling at Jesus' feet; he was a Samaritan
Jesus asked, 'Were there not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?' He said, 'Your faith has made you well'
Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, sat by the road; hearing it was Jesus, he cried, 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!'
Many warned him to be quiet, but he cried out all the more
Jesus asked what he wanted; he said 'that I may receive my sight.' Jesus said, 'Go; your faith has made you well.' Immediately he received his sight
One of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear; Jesus touched his ear and healed him
Peter was the one who struck; the servant's name was Malchus
Jesus said, 'Put your sword in its place... Do you think I cannot now pray to My Father, and He will provide Me with more than twelve legions of angels?'

question: What was healing the lame man at the Beautiful Gate?

scripture_says.text: A man lame from birth was laid daily at the temple gate called Beautiful to ask alms
Peter said, 'Silver and gold I do not have, but what I have I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ, rise up and walk'
He leaped up, stood, and walked, entering the temple walking, leaping, and praising God
The man was over forty years old

history_records.text: The Beautiful Gate was likely one of the eastern entrances to the temple complex

question: What was the death of Ananias and Sapphira?

scripture_says.text: Ananias sold property but kept back part of the price with his wife's knowledge; Peter said he had lied to the Holy Spirit
Ananias fell down and died; great fear came upon all who heard
Peter told Sapphira the feet of those who buried her husband were at the door; she fell dead at his feet
Great fear came upon all the church and all who heard these things

question: What was the apostles' release from prison?

scripture_says.text: They laid hands on the apostles and put them in the common prison; an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors at night
The angel said, 'Go, stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this life'
The officers found the prison securely shut with guards standing, but no one inside
Peter was later similarly released from Herod's prison by an angel

question: What was the healing of Aeneas?

scripture_says.text: Aeneas had been bedridden eight years, paralyzed; Peter said, 'Jesus the Christ heals you. Arise and make your bed'
He arose immediately
All who dwelt at Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord

history_records.text: Lydda (modern Lod) is located in the coastal plain of Israel

question: What was the raising of Dorcas?

scripture_says.text: Dorcas (Tabitha), full of good works, became sick and died; they washed her and laid her in an upper room
Peter put everyone out, knelt, and prayed; turning to the body he said, 'Tabitha, arise.' She opened her eyes and sat up
He presented her alive; it became known throughout Joppa and many believed on the Lord

history_records.text: Joppa (modern Jaffa) is an ancient port city on the Mediterranean coast

question: What was the blinding of Elymas?

scripture_says.text: Elymas the sorcerer opposed Paul and Barnabas, trying to turn the proconsul away from the faith
Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, said, 'You son of the devil... you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a time'
Immediately mist and darkness fell on him; the proconsul believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord

history_records.text: This occurred in Paphos, Cyprus, before the proconsul Sergius Paulus

question: What was healing the cripple at Lystra?

scripture_says.text: A man in Lystra, crippled from birth, had never walked; Paul observed he had faith to be healed
Paul said with a loud voice, 'Stand up straight on your feet!' He leaped and walked
The crowds said the gods had come down; they called Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes, and tried to sacrifice to them

history_records.text: Lystra was a Roman colony in the region of Lycaonia (modern Turkey)

question: What was the raising of Eutychus?

scripture_says.text: Eutychus, sitting in a window, sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked; he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead
Paul went down, fell on him, and embraced him, saying, 'Do not trouble yourselves, for his life is in him'
They brought the young man alive and were greatly comforted

history_records.text: This occurred in Troas during Paul's third missionary journey

question: What was Paul and the viper on Malta?

scripture_says.text: When Paul gathered sticks, a viper came out because of the heat and fastened on his hand; the natives expected him to swell up and die
He shook off the creature into the fire and suffered no harm; they changed their minds and said he was a god
Paul healed Publius' father and others on the island who had diseases

history_records.text: Malta (Melita) is an island in the central Mediterranean

scripture_says.text: God spoke all these words: no other gods, no idols, not take the Lord's name in vain, remember the Sabbath, honor parents, no murder, no adultery, no stealing, no false witness, no coveting
Moses recounts the Ten Commandments to the new generation before entering Canaan
God gave Moses two tablets of the Testimony, written with the finger of God
Moses wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments

history_records.text: The Hebrew term is literally 'Ten Words' (עֲשֶׂרֶת הַדְּבָרִים)
Given at Mount Sinai approximately 1446 BC (or 1290 BC in alternative chronology)

scripture_says.text: You shall have no other gods before Me
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one! You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, soul, and strength
Jesus said this is the first and great commandment

history_records.text: The Shema (Deu.6.4-5) became central to Jewish daily prayer and practice

scripture_says.text: You shall not make for yourself a carved image or any likeness; you shall not bow down to them or serve them
Take careful heed, for you saw no form when the LORD spoke; lest you make a carved image
To whom then will you liken God? Or what likeness will you compare to Him?
You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not hold him guiltless who takes His name in vain
You shall not swear by My name falsely, nor shall you profane the name of your God
Jesus taught not to swear at all, but let your 'Yes' be 'Yes' and your 'No,' 'No'

history_records.text: Jewish tradition avoids speaking God's name (YHWH), substituting Adonai or HaShem

scripture_says.text: Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy. Six days you shall labor; the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD your God
The Sabbath is a sign between God and Israel forever, for in six days the LORD made heaven and earth
Jesus said, 'The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath'

history_records.text: The Sabbath runs from Friday sunset to Saturday sunset in Jewish practice

scripture_says.text: Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long upon the land
Paul calls this the first commandment with promise
Jesus criticized traditions that allowed neglecting parents through 'Corban'
You shall not murder
Jesus said whoever is angry with his brother without cause is in danger of judgment
Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and no murderer has eternal life abiding in him

history_records.text: The Hebrew word ratsach refers to unlawful killing; the law distinguishes murder from manslaughter (Num.35.16-25)

scripture_says.text: You shall not commit adultery
Jesus said whoever looks at a woman to lust for her has already committed adultery in his heart
Marriage is honorable among all; fornicators and adulterers God will judge
You shall not steal
Let him who stole steal no longer, but rather let him labor with his hands
You shall not steal, nor deal falsely, nor lie to one another
You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor
A lying tongue and a false witness who speaks lies are among the things the LORD hates
Do not lie to one another, since you have put off the old man with his deeds
You shall not covet your neighbor's house, wife, servant, ox, donkey, or anything that is your neighbor's
Paul said he would not have known sin except through the Law, for the law said 'You shall not covet'
The love of money is a root of all kinds of evil

question: What is the command to love your neighbor?

scripture_says.text: You shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the LORD
Jesus said the second great commandment is like the first: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself'
Love does no harm to a neighbor; love is the fulfillment of the law
All the law is fulfilled in one word: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself'

question: What is the command to love the stranger?

scripture_says.text: If a stranger dwells with you, you shall not mistreat him; you shall love him as yourself, for you were strangers in Egypt
God loves the stranger, giving him food and clothing. Therefore love the stranger, for you were strangers in Egypt
You shall neither mistreat a stranger nor oppress him
Among animals: whatever divides the hoof and chews the cud, you may eat
Of sea creatures: whatever has fins and scales you may eat
Certain birds are declared unclean: eagle, vulture, buzzard, and others
Peter's vision: 'What God has cleansed you must not call common'

history_records.text: These laws distinguished Israel from surrounding nations (Lev.20.25-26)

question: What is the command of circumcision?

scripture_says.text: Every male child shall be circumcised at eight days old; it shall be a sign of the covenant
On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised
Abraham received circumcision as a sign, a seal of the righteousness of faith he had while uncircumcised
In Christ you were circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, buried with Him in baptism

history_records.text: The Jerusalem Council (Acts 15) determined Gentile believers did not need circumcision

scripture_says.text: The burnt offering: a male without blemish, laid hands on its head for atonement
The sin offering: if the anointed priest sins, he shall bring a young bull without blemish
The peace offering: thanksgiving, votive, or freewill offering
The law was a shadow of good things to come; it is not possible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins

history_records.text: Five main offerings: burnt (olah), grain (minchah), peace (shelamim), sin (chattath), guilt (asham)

question: What was the Day of Atonement?

scripture_says.text: On the tenth day of the seventh month you shall afflict your souls and do no work; it is a Sabbath of solemn rest
The high priest shall make atonement for the Holy Place, the tabernacle, and the altar
The scapegoat shall bear all their iniquities to an uninhabited land
Christ entered the Most Holy Place once for all, not with the blood of goats and calves, but with His own blood

history_records.text: Yom Kippur (Day of Atonement) remains the holiest day in the Jewish calendar

scripture_says.text: Six years you shall sow your field; in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest for the land
At the end of every seven years you shall grant a release of debts
The land enjoyed its sabbaths during the seventy years of exile, as long as it lay desolate

history_records.text: The failure to observe sabbatical years is cited as a reason for the exile's length (Jer.25.11-12)

question: What was the Year of Jubilee?

scripture_says.text: You shall consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants
In this Year of Jubilee, each of you shall return to his possession
The land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; you are strangers and sojourners with Me
To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD - quoted by Jesus in Luke 4.18-19

history_records.text: Liberty Bell inscription is from Leviticus 25.10

question: What were the cities of refuge?

scripture_says.text: You shall appoint cities of refuge, that the manslayer who kills any person accidentally may flee there
They appointed Kedesh, Shechem, and Hebron on the west; Bezer, Ramoth, and Golan on the east
The manslayer shall stay in the city of refuge until the death of the high priest
We have fled for refuge to lay hold of the hope set before us

history_records.text: Six cities provided protection from blood avengers until a fair trial could occur

question: What was the law of gleaning?

scripture_says.text: When you reap, you shall not wholly reap the corners of your field, nor gather the gleanings; leave them for the poor and stranger
You shall leave them for the poor and for the stranger: I am the LORD your God
Ruth went and gleaned in the field after the reapers, in the field belonging to Boaz

history_records.text: The book of Ruth demonstrates the gleaning law in practice during the judges period

question: What was the law of firstfruits?

scripture_says.text: The first of the firstfruits of your land you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God
When you come into the land and reap its harvest, bring a sheaf of the firstfruits to the priest
Christ is risen from the dead, and has become the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep
We ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, groan within ourselves, waiting for adoption

question: What was the law of tithing?

scripture_says.text: All the tithe of the land, whether of the seed or fruit of the tree, is the LORD's; it is holy to the LORD
To the Levites I have given every tithe in Israel as an inheritance for their service
'Bring all the tithes into the storehouse... And try Me now,' says the LORD
Jesus said they tithe mint, anise, and cummin, but neglect justice, mercy, and faith; these ought to be done without leaving the others undone

history_records.text: Abraham gave tithes to Melchizedek (Gen.14.20), predating the Mosaic law

scripture_says.text: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all things I have commanded you
Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature
Repentance and remission of sins should be preached in His name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem
You shall be witnesses to Me in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth
A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another; as I have loved you, that you also love one another. By this all will know that you are My disciples
This is My commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you
This is His commandment: that we should believe on the name of His Son Jesus Christ and love one another
This commandment we have from Him: that he who loves God must love his brother also

scripture_says.text: This is the disciple who testifies of these things... we know that his testimony is true
One of His disciples, whom Jesus loved, was leaning on Jesus' bosom
He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true

history_records.text: The 'beloved disciple' is traditionally identified as John son of Zebedee
Irenaeus (c. AD 180) states John wrote the Gospel from Ephesus
Early church fathers consistently attributed the Gospel to John

scripture_says.text: Paul, a bondservant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle
Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ
See with what large letters I have written to you with my own hand
The salutation of Paul with my own hand, which is a sign in every epistle; so I write

history_records.text: Thirteen letters bear Paul's name: Romans through Philemon
Paul often used an amanuensis (secretary) but added personal greetings (Rom.16.22)
The Prison Epistles (Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, Philemon) were written during imprisonment

scripture_says.text: Know that our brother Timothy has been set free, with whom I shall see you if he comes shortly
Which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed to us by those who heard Him

history_records.text: The author is not identified; early church had various theories
Suggestions include Paul, Barnabas, Apollos, Luke, Clement, or Priscilla
Origen (c. AD 225) wrote: 'Who wrote the epistle, in truth God knows'
The author was familiar with the readers and with Timothy

scripture_says.text: James, a bondservant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad
I saw none of the other apostles except James, the Lord's brother
James answered, saying, 'Men and brethren, listen to me'

history_records.text: Traditionally attributed to James the Just, brother of Jesus (Mat.13.55)
James led the Jerusalem church (Act.15, Gal.2.9)
Josephus records James was martyred in AD 62

scripture_says.text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ
Simon Peter, a bondservant and apostle of Jesus Christ
We were eyewitnesses of His majesty... we heard this voice which came from heaven when we were with Him on the holy mountain
By Silvanus, our faithful brother... I have written to you briefly

history_records.text: Peter used Silvanus (Silas) as his secretary for 1 Peter
The style difference between 1 and 2 Peter may reflect different secretaries
Peter's martyrdom under Nero (c. AD 64-67) gives a terminus for his letters

scripture_says.text: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon... we declare to you
The Elder, to the elect lady and her children
The Elder, to the beloved Gaius

history_records.text: The author of 2 and 3 John identifies himself as 'the Elder'
The vocabulary and themes closely match the Gospel of John
Early church fathers attributed these letters to the apostle John

scripture_says.text: Jude, a bondservant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James
Is not His mother called Mary? And His brothers James, Joses, Simon, and Judas?

history_records.text: Jude was a brother of Jesus and of James (who led the Jerusalem church)
He calls himself 'brother of James' rather than 'brother of Jesus' out of humility
The letter was accepted as canonical by the early church

scripture_says.text: The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave Him to show His servants—things which must shortly take place
John, to the seven churches which are in Asia
I, John, both your brother and companion in the tribulation... was on the island called Patmos

history_records.text: Early church (Justin Martyr, Irenaeus) identified the author as the apostle John
John was exiled to Patmos under persecution
The writing style differs from John's Gospel, possibly due to different secretaries or circumstances

question: What are the Dead Sea Scrolls?

scripture_says.text: The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God stands forever

history_records.text: Discovered 1947-1956 in caves near Qumran by the Dead Sea
Include manuscripts of every OT book except Esther, dated 250 BC - AD 68
The Great Isaiah Scroll (1QIsaᵃ) contains the entire book of Isaiah, virtually identical to later texts
Confirm remarkable textual preservation over 1,000 years

scripture_says.text: Cyrus king of Persia made a proclamation: The LORD God of heaven has commanded me to build Him a house at Jerusalem
Who says of Cyrus, 'He is My shepherd, and he shall perform all My pleasure, saying to Jerusalem, You shall be built'

history_records.text: Discovered in Babylon in 1879, now in the British Museum
Dated to 539 BC, describes Cyrus's conquest of Babylon
Records Cyrus's policy of returning exiled peoples and their gods to their homelands
Confirms the biblical account of Cyrus's decree allowing Jews to return

question: What is the Tel Dan Stele?

scripture_says.text: Your house and your kingdom shall be established forever before you. Your throne shall be established forever
So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day

history_records.text: Discovered at Tel Dan in 1993-1994
An Aramean victory stele from the 9th century BC
Contains the phrase 'House of David' (bytdwd) - the earliest extra-biblical reference to David
Confirms David as the founder of Judah's ruling dynasty

scripture_says.text: In the fifteenth year of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea
They bound Him, led Him away, and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor

history_records.text: Discovered at Caesarea Maritima in 1961
Latin inscription mentions 'Pontius Pilatus, Prefect of Judea'
Confirms Pilate's existence and title during the time of Jesus
Now housed in the Israel Museum, Jerusalem

scripture_says.text: Hezekiah... how he made a pool and a tunnel and brought water into the city
This same Hezekiah also stopped the water outlet of Upper Gihon, and brought the water by tunnel to the west side of the City of David

history_records.text: A 1,750-foot tunnel cut through bedrock in Jerusalem, connecting the Gihon Spring to the Pool of Siloam
The Siloam Inscription, found in 1880, describes workers meeting in the middle
Built c. 701 BC to secure Jerusalem's water supply before Sennacherib's siege

scripture_says.text: In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah
The angel of the LORD went out and struck the camp of the Assyrians... Sennacherib departed and went home

history_records.text: Discovered at Nineveh in 1830, now in the British Museum
Sennacherib boasts of besieging 46 cities of Judah and shutting up Hezekiah 'like a bird in a cage'
Notably does NOT claim to have captured Jerusalem - matching the biblical account
Confirms the Assyrian campaign described in 2 Kings 18-19

question: What is the Moabite Stone (Mesha Stele)?

scripture_says.text: Mesha king of Moab was a sheepbreeder... When Ahab died, the king of Moab rebelled against the king of Israel
He took his eldest son who would have reigned in his place, and offered him as a burnt offering upon the wall

history_records.text: Discovered in 1868 at Dhiban, Jordan (ancient Dibon of Moab)
Dated to c. 840 BC, written by King Mesha of Moab
Mentions the 'House of Omri' (Israel), Chemosh (Moab's god), and possibly YHWH
Provides Moabite perspective on events in 2 Kings 3
Discovered in 1799 in Rosetta (Rashid), Egypt
Contains a decree in three scripts: hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Greek
Enabled decipherment of Egyptian hieroglyphics by Jean-François Champollion in 1822
Unlocked understanding of Egyptian texts relevant to biblical history

question: What is the Black Obelisk of Shalmaneser III?

scripture_says.text: In those days the LORD began to cut off parts of Israel; and Hazael conquered them in all the territory of Israel
Elisha sent a prophet to anoint Jehu king over Israel

history_records.text: Discovered at Nimrud in 1846, now in the British Museum
Dated to 841 BC, depicts tribute from various kings
Shows 'Jehu, son of Omri' bowing before Shalmaneser III
Contains the only contemporary depiction of an Israelite king

scripture_says.text: Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and took them
The king of Assyria sent the Tartan, Rabsaris, and Rabshakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah

history_records.text: Carved stone panels from Sennacherib's palace at Nineveh, now in the British Museum
Depict the Assyrian siege and capture of Lachish in 701 BC
Show siege ramps, battering rams, deportation of captives, and impalement of prisoners
Archaeological excavations at Tel Lachish confirm the Assyrian siege

scripture_says.text: That very night Belshazzar, king of the Chaldeans, was slain. And Darius the Mede received the kingdom
Belshazzar the king made a great feast for a thousand of his lords

history_records.text: A Babylonian clay tablet recording the fall of Babylon to Cyrus in 539 BC
Confirms Belshazzar was acting king while his father Nabonidus was away
Records that Babylon fell without a major battle, matching Daniel's account
Now in the British Museum
Discovered at Tell Mardikh, Syria, in 1974-1975
Approximately 20,000 cuneiform tablets dated to c. 2400-2300 BC
Contain personal names similar to biblical patriarchal names
Reference cities mentioned in Genesis including Sodom, Gomorrah, and Zoar
Demonstrate that writing and complex civilization existed in the patriarchal period

scripture_says.text: Abram said, 'The heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus'
Rachel had stolen the household idols that were her father's

history_records.text: Discovered at Nuzi (modern Yorghan Tepe, Iraq) in 1925-1931
Approximately 5,000 tablets from the 15th-14th century BC Hurrian culture
Describe adoption customs matching Abraham adopting Eliezer
Explain significance of household gods (teraphim) for inheritance rights
Illuminate patriarchal customs in Genesis
Discovered at Tell Hariri, Syria (ancient Mari) from 1933 onward
Over 20,000 cuneiform tablets from the 18th century BC
Mention cities and tribes with names similar to biblical patriarchs (Nahor, Serug, Peleg)
Describe prophetic practices and pastoral nomadism similar to Genesis accounts
Provide context for the world of Abraham's time
Discovered at Thebes, Egypt in 1896, now in the Cairo Museum
Dated to c. 1208 BC, during reign of Pharaoh Merneptah
Contains the earliest extra-biblical reference to Israel: 'Israel is laid waste; his seed is not'
Confirms Israel existed as a people in Canaan by the late 13th century BC
Also called the 'Israel Stele'
Discovered at Tell el-Amarna, Egypt in 1887
382 cuneiform tablets, diplomatic correspondence from 14th century BC
Include letters from Canaanite kings to Egyptian pharaohs
Mention 'Habiru' or 'Apiru' people causing unrest in Canaan
Some scholars connect these to the Hebrew conquest, though the identification is debated
Describe conditions in Canaan before or during Israelite settlement

scripture_says.text: The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews who dwell in the land of Egypt

history_records.text: Discovered on Elephantine Island in the Nile, Egypt, late 19th century
Aramaic documents from a Jewish military colony, 5th century BC
Include letters to the Persian governor mentioning the high priest in Jerusalem
Reference a Jewish temple at Elephantine (contrary to Deuteronomy's single sanctuary)
Provide insight into Jewish life during the Persian period

scripture_says.text: Hezekiah made a pool and a tunnel and brought water into the city
Hezekiah stopped the water outlet of Upper Gihon, and brought the water by tunnel

history_records.text: Discovered in 1880 inside Hezekiah's Tunnel in Jerusalem
A Hebrew inscription describing how two teams of workers met in the middle
Dated to c. 701 BC during Hezekiah's preparations for Assyrian siege
Now in the Istanbul Archaeological Museum
One of the oldest Hebrew inscriptions ever found

question: What are the Ketef Hinnom Silver Scrolls?

scripture_says.text: The LORD bless you and keep you; the LORD make His face shine upon you and be gracious to you; the LORD lift up His countenance upon you and give you peace

history_records.text: Discovered in 1979 in burial caves at Ketef Hinnom, Jerusalem
Two tiny silver scrolls containing the priestly blessing (Numbers 6.24-26)
Dated to the late 7th century BC (pre-exilic)
Oldest surviving texts of the Hebrew Bible, predating Dead Sea Scrolls by 400 years
Now in the Israel Museum, Jerusalem

question: What is the Seal of Hezekiah?

scripture_says.text: Hezekiah the son of Ahaz, king of Judah, began to reign... He trusted in the LORD God of Israel

history_records.text: Clay seal impression (bulla) discovered in Jerusalem in 2015
Inscription reads: 'Belonging to Hezekiah [son of] Ahaz, king of Judah'
Bears a winged sun disk symbol
First seal impression of an Israelite or Judean king found in controlled excavation
Confirms the biblical account of King Hezekiah

question: What is the possible Seal of Isaiah?

scripture_says.text: Hezekiah sent Eliakim, Shebna, and the elders of the priests to Isaiah the prophet
The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz

history_records.text: Clay seal impression discovered near Hezekiah's seal in Jerusalem, 2018
Inscription reads: 'Belonging to Isaiah nvy' (possibly 'prophet')
The second word is partially damaged; 'prophet' interpretation is debated
If authentic, would be first archaeological evidence of the prophet Isaiah
Found just 10 feet from Hezekiah's seal impression

scripture_says.text: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came against the city... Jehoiachin king of Judah, surrendered
Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem

history_records.text: Series of clay tablets recording Babylonian history, now in the British Museum
Chronicle for 605-594 BC describes Nebuchadnezzar's campaigns against Judah
Records the capture of Jerusalem in March 597 BC, matching 2 Kings 24
States Nebuchadnezzar 'seized the city and captured the king' (Jehoiachin)
Provides precise dating for biblical events

question: What are the Jehoiachin Ration Tablets?

scripture_says.text: He carried Jehoiachin captive to Babylon
Evil-Merodach king of Babylon, in the year that he began to reign, released Jehoiachin... and gave him a regular allowance

history_records.text: Cuneiform tablets discovered at Babylon, dated to 592 BC
Administrative records listing rations given to captives in Babylon
Mention 'Jehoiachin, king of Judah' and his five sons
Confirm Jehoiachin was held in Babylon and received provisions
Now in the Pergamon Museum, Berlin

question: What is the Pool of Siloam?

scripture_says.text: Jesus said to the blind man, 'Go, wash in the pool of Siloam.' He went and washed, and came back seeing
He answered, 'A Man called Jesus made clay and anointed my eyes and said to me, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash'

history_records.text: Excavated in 2004 in the City of David, Jerusalem
A large stepped pool from the Second Temple period
Much larger than previously known (225 feet long)
Pilgrims would immerse here before ascending to the Temple
Confirms the Gospel of John's geographical accuracy

scripture_says.text: Those who had laid hold of Jesus led Him away to Caiaphas the high priest
Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said... 'it is expedient for us that one man should die for the people'

history_records.text: Discovered in 1990 in a burial cave south of Jerusalem
Ornate limestone ossuary (bone box) inscribed 'Yehosef bar Qafa' (Joseph son of Caiaphas)
Contained bones of a 60-year-old man, likely the high priest Caiaphas
Now in the Israel Museum, Jerusalem
First archaeological evidence of a New Testament figure from the passion narrative

scripture_says.text: Is not His mother called Mary? And His brothers James, Joses, Simon, and Judas?
I saw none of the other apostles except James, the Lord's brother

history_records.text: Surfaced in 2002, allegedly from the Jerusalem area
Aramaic inscription reads: 'James, son of Joseph, brother of Jesus'
If authentic, would be first archaeological reference to Jesus of Nazareth
Authenticity has been debated; owner was acquitted of forgery charges in 2012
Geological analysis supports 1st-century origin; inscription's authenticity remains disputed

question: What is the Crucified Man of Giv'at HaMivtar?

scripture_says.text: There they crucified Him, and two others with Him, one on either side, and Jesus in the center
The soldiers broke the legs of the first and of the other... But when they came to Jesus and saw that He was already dead, they did not break His legs

history_records.text: Discovered in 1968 in a tomb at Giv'at HaMivtar, Jerusalem
Ossuary contained bones of a crucified man named Yehohanan
A nail was still embedded through his heel bone
Dated to the 1st century AD, contemporary with Jesus
Only archaeological evidence of Roman crucifixion ever found
Confirms Gospel accounts of crucifixion practices

scripture_says.text: The chief priests said, 'Tell them, His disciples came at night and stole Him away while we slept'
The chief priests and Pharisees went to Pilate saying, 'Command that the tomb be made secure'

history_records.text: A marble tablet acquired from Nazareth in 1878, now in Paris
Contains a Greek imperial decree threatening death for tomb robbery
Dated to the reign of Claudius (AD 41-54) or possibly earlier
Some scholars suggest it may be a response to claims about Jesus' resurrection
Would indicate Roman awareness of the empty tomb controversy

scripture_says.text: When Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul and brought him to the judgment seat

history_records.text: Stone fragments discovered at Delphi, Greece, in early 20th century
Contains a letter from Emperor Claudius mentioning Gallio as proconsul of Achaia
Dates Gallio's term to AD 51-52
Provides a fixed point for dating Paul's ministry and his letters
Confirms the accuracy of Acts' historical details

scripture_says.text: Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you
Erastus stayed in Corinth

history_records.text: A paving stone discovered at Corinth in 1929
Latin inscription reads: 'Erastus in return for his aedileship laid [this pavement] at his own expense'
Dated to mid-1st century AD
An aedile managed public works and finances, matching Paul's description of 'treasurer'
Possibly the same Erastus mentioned by Paul in Romans

scripture_says.text: They dragged Jason and some brethren to the rulers of the city (politarchs), crying out, 'These who have turned the world upside down have come here too'

history_records.text: Multiple inscriptions from Thessalonica using the term 'politarch'
Over 30 inscriptions found using this title for city officials
Luke's use of 'politarchs' was once questioned as unhistorical
The inscriptions vindicate Luke's accuracy for Macedonian terminology
The Vardar Gate inscription (now in the British Museum) is a key example

question: What is the law of 'eye for eye'?

scripture_says.text: You shall give life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot, burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe
If a man causes disfigurement of his neighbor, as he has done, so shall it be done to him
Jesus said, 'You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye... But I tell you not to resist an evil person'

history_records.text: Known as lex talionis (law of retaliation), this principle limited vengeance to proportional response
Similar laws appear in the Code of Hammurabi (c. 1754 BC)

question: What is the law against false witness?

scripture_says.text: If a false witness rises against any man... then you shall do to him as he thought to have done to his brother
One witness shall not rise against a man concerning any iniquity... by the mouth of two or three witnesses the matter shall be established
A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who speaks lies will not escape

question: What is the law about landmarks?

scripture_says.text: You shall not remove your neighbor's landmark, which the men of old have set, in your inheritance
Cursed is the one who moves his neighbor's landmark. And all the people shall say, Amen!
Do not remove the ancient landmark which your fathers have set

history_records.text: Boundary stones marked land inheritances; moving them was theft of ancestral property

question: What is the law about weights and measures?

scripture_says.text: You shall do no injustice in judgment, in measurement of length, weight, or volume. You shall have honest scales, honest weights
You shall not have in your bag differing weights, a heavy and a light... For all who do such things, all who behave unrighteously, are an abomination to the LORD
Dishonest scales are an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is His delight

history_records.text: Merchants would use heavier weights when buying and lighter weights when selling to cheat customers

question: What is the law about paying wages?

scripture_says.text: You shall not defraud your neighbor, nor rob him. The wages of him who is hired shall not remain with you all night until morning
You shall not oppress a hired servant who is poor... Each day you shall give him his wages, and not let the sun go down on it
The wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out

history_records.text: Day laborers depended on daily wages for that day's food

question: What is the law about widows and orphans?

scripture_says.text: You shall not afflict any widow or fatherless child. If you afflict them in any way, and they cry at all to Me, I will surely hear their cry
You shall not pervert justice due the stranger or the fatherless, nor take a widow's garment as a pledge
Pure and undefiled religion before God... is this: to visit orphans and widows in their trouble

history_records.text: Without a male provider, widows and orphans were among the most vulnerable in ancient society

question: What is the law about usury (interest)?

scripture_says.text: If you lend money to any of My people who are poor among you, you shall not be like a moneylender to him; you shall not charge him interest
If one of your brethren becomes poor... you shall not charge him interest or usury
You shall not charge interest to your brother... To a foreigner you may charge interest, but to your brother you shall not charge interest

history_records.text: Interest-bearing loans to fellow Israelites were prohibited to prevent exploitation of the poor

question: What is the law about pledges?

scripture_says.text: If you take your neighbor's garment as a pledge, you shall return it to him before the sun goes down... for that is his only covering
No man shall take the lower or the upper millstone in pledge, for he takes one's living in pledge
When you lend your brother anything, you shall not go into his house to get his pledge... If the man is poor, you shall not keep his pledge overnight

history_records.text: Pledges (collateral) could not include essential items needed for daily survival

scripture_says.text: If brothers dwell together, and one dies without a son, the wife shall not be married outside to a stranger; her husband's brother shall take her as wife... the firstborn shall succeed to the name of the dead brother
Judah said to Onan, 'Go in to your brother's wife and marry her, and raise up an heir to your brother'
The Sadducees questioned Jesus about a woman who had seven brothers as husbands

history_records.text: The word 'levirate' comes from Latin levir meaning 'husband's brother'
This law preserved the deceased's name and inheritance in Israel

question: What is the law about divorce?

scripture_says.text: When a man takes a wife... and he finds some uncleanness in her, then let him write her a certificate of divorce... she may go and be another man's wife
Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts, permitted you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so
'For the LORD God of Israel says that He hates divorce'

history_records.text: Rabbinical schools debated what 'uncleanness' permitted divorce - some said only adultery, others any displeasing thing

question: What were the qualifications for priests?

scripture_says.text: No man of your descendants who has any defect may approach to offer the bread of his God
They shall not take a wife who is a harlot or a defiled woman, nor shall they take a woman divorced from her husband
The high priest... shall not uncover his head nor tear his clothes... nor go out of the sanctuary

history_records.text: Priests had to be descendants of Aaron, physically unblemished, and ceremonially pure

scripture_says.text: When a man or woman consecrates himself to take the vow of a Nazirite... he shall separate himself from wine and similar drink... no razor shall come upon his head... he shall not go near a dead body
The child shall be a Nazirite to God from the womb (Samson)
We have four men who have taken a vow... take them and be purified with them

history_records.text: Nazirite comes from Hebrew nazir meaning 'consecrated' or 'separated'
Famous Nazirites include Samson, Samuel, and John the Baptist

scripture_says.text: Bring you a red heifer without blemish, in which there is no defect and on which a yoke has never come... it shall be burned
A man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer... it shall be kept for the congregation for the water of purification
If the ashes of a heifer, sprinkling the unclean, sanctifies for the purifying of the flesh, how much more shall the blood of Christ... cleanse your conscience

history_records.text: Tradition says only nine red heifers were burned from Moses to the temple's destruction
The ashes mixed with water purified those who had touched a dead body

scripture_says.text: You shall set the showbread on the table before Me always
You shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes... Every Sabbath he shall set it in order before the LORD continually
Jesus said, 'Have you not read what David did when he was hungry... how he entered the house of God and ate the showbread?'

history_records.text: Also called 'bread of the Presence' - twelve loaves representing the twelve tribes
Only priests could eat it, replaced every Sabbath

question: What is the altar of incense?

scripture_says.text: You shall make an altar to burn incense on... Aaron shall burn on it sweet incense every morning and evening
Take sweet spices... and pure frankincense... You shall make of these an incense... Whoever makes any like it, to smell it, shall be cut off
The smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the saints, ascended before God from the angel's hand

history_records.text: The incense was a specific blend of four spices, reserved only for sacred use

question: What is the law against blasphemy?

scripture_says.text: Whoever curses his God shall bear his sin. And whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death
You shall not revile God, nor curse a ruler of your people
The high priest tore his clothes, saying, 'He has spoken blasphemy!'

history_records.text: Blasphemy of God's name was a capital offense in Israel
Jesus was accused of blasphemy for claiming divine authority

question: What is the law about eating blood?

scripture_says.text: For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it to you upon the altar to make atonement for your souls... No one among you shall eat blood
But you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood
Abstain from things offered to idols, from blood, from things strangled, and from sexual immorality

history_records.text: Blood represented life and was reserved for atonement on the altar

question: What is the law about vows?

scripture_says.text: If a man makes a vow to the LORD, or swears an oath to bind himself by some agreement, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceeds out of his mouth
When you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to pay it... what has gone from your lips you shall keep and perform
When you make a vow to God, do not delay to pay it... Better not to vow than to vow and not pay

question: What are the laws of uncleanness?

scripture_says.text: By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until evening
When any man has a discharge from his body, his discharge is unclean
He who touches the dead body of anyone shall be unclean seven days

history_records.text: Uncleanness was not sinfulness but a ritual state that prevented temple access
Purification involved washing, time, and sometimes sacrifice

question: What are the laws about mixing kinds?

scripture_says.text: You shall not let your livestock breed with another kind. You shall not sow your field with mixed seed. Nor shall a garment of mixed linen and wool come upon you

You shall not sow your vineyard with different kinds of seed... You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey together. You shall not wear a garment of different sorts, such as wool and linen mixed together

history_records.text: These laws may have taught Israel to maintain distinctions as a holy, separate nation
The priestly garments were an exception, using linen and possibly dyed wool

scripture_says.text: Work shall be done for six days, but the seventh day shall be a holy day for you... You shall kindle no fire throughout your dwellings on the Sabbath day
They found a man gathering sticks on the Sabbath day... the LORD said to Moses, 'The man must surely be put to death'
In those days I saw people treading wine presses on the Sabbath, and bringing in sheaves... and I warned them about the day on which they were selling provisions

history_records.text: Rabbinical tradition later defined 39 categories of prohibited Sabbath work

question: What is the prophecy of being born of a woman?

scripture_says.text: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your seed and her Seed
When the fullness of the time had come, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under the law
Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a Son, and shall call His name Immanuel

history_records.text: The phrase 'her Seed' is unusual - seed typically refers to the male line

question: What is the prophecy of the shepherd being struck?

scripture_says.text: Awake, O sword, against My Shepherd, against the Man who is My Companion, says the LORD of hosts. Strike the Shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered
Jesus said to them, 'All of you will be made to stumble because of Me this night, for it is written: I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered'

question: What is the prophecy of the potter's field?

scripture_says.text: So they weighed out for my wages thirty pieces of silver. And the LORD said to me, 'Throw it to the potter'
The chief priests took the silver pieces... and bought with them the potter's field... Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet

history_records.text: Matthew attributes this to Jeremiah, though the passage is in Zechariah; this may reference Jeremiah's purchase of a field (Jer.32.6-9) combined with Zechariah's prophecy

question: What is the prophecy of the messenger preparing the way?

Can you draw out Leviathan with a hook, or snare his tongue with a line which you lower?
So God created great sea creatures and every living thing that moves
Then God said, 'Let the earth bring forth the living creature according to its kind: cattle and creeping thing and beast of the earth'

history_records.text: The word 'dinosaur' was coined in 1842 by Richard Owen
The Hebrew word 'tanniyn' (often translated 'dragon' or 'sea monster') appears 28 times in the OT

scripture_says.text: There were giants (Nephilim) on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men and they bore children to them. Those were the mighty men who were of old, men of renown
There we saw the giants (the descendants of Anak came from the giants); and we were like grasshoppers in our own sight, and so we were in their sight

history_records.text: The word 'Nephilim' comes from Hebrew 'naphal' meaning 'to fall'
Ancient interpreters debated whether 'sons of God' refers to angels, the line of Seth, or rulers
1 Enoch (Jewish pseudepigrapha) describes the Nephilim as offspring of fallen angels

scripture_says.text: When the Day of Pentecost had fully come, they were all with one accord in one place. And suddenly there came a sound from heaven, as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting. Then there appeared to them divided tongues, as of fire, and one sat upon each of them. And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance
Everyone heard them speak in his own language. Then they were all amazed and marveled, saying... 'How is it that we hear, each in our own language in which we were born?'
Those who gladly received his word were baptized; and that day about three thousand souls were added to them

history_records.text: Pentecost was a Jewish harvest festival (Feast of Weeks), 50 days after Passover
Jews from throughout the Roman world gathered in Jerusalem for the festival

scripture_says.text: As he journeyed he came near Damascus, and suddenly a light shone around him from heaven. Then he fell to the ground, and heard a voice saying to him, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?' And he said, 'Who are You, Lord?' Then the Lord said, 'I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting'
As I journeyed and came near Damascus at about noon, suddenly a great light from heaven shone around me
We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice speaking to me and saying in the Hebrew language, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?'

history_records.text: Saul/Paul was a Pharisee and persecutor of the early church (Act 8:1-3)
His conversion is dated to approximately AD 33-36

scripture_says.text: The Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel, and with the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first. Then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air
Behold, I tell you a mystery: We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed—in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet
Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left

history_records.text: The term 'rapture' comes from Latin 'rapturo,' translating Greek 'harpazo' (caught up)
Pre-tribulation rapture theology was popularized in the 19th century through John Nelson Darby

scripture_says.text: Then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the world until this time, no, nor ever shall be
These are the ones who come out of the great tribulation, and washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb
There shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation, even to that time
It was granted to him to make war with the saints and to overcome them

history_records.text: Christians have debated whether the tribulation is past, present, or future
Daniel's 70th week (Dan 9:27) is often connected to the tribulation period

scripture_says.text: I saw an angel coming down from heaven, having the key to the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand. He laid hold of the dragon, that serpent of old, who is the Devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years... And I saw thrones, and they sat on them, and judgment was committed to them... And they lived and reigned with Christ for a thousand years
Blessed and holy is he who has part in the first resurrection. Over such the second death has no power, but they shall be priests of God and of Christ, and shall reign with Him a thousand years

history_records.text: Major views include premillennialism, amillennialism, and postmillennialism
Early church fathers including Justin Martyr and Irenaeus held premillennial views

scripture_says.text: Little children, it is the last hour; and as you have heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have come, by which we know that it is the last hour

Let no one deceive you by any means; for that Day will not come unless the falling away comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of perdition, who opposes and exalts himself above all that is called God or that is worshiped
I saw a beast rising up out of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns... The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority
Every spirit that does not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is not of God. And this is the spirit of the Antichrist

history_records.text: Various historical figures have been identified as the Antichrist throughout history
The term 'antichrist' appears only in John's epistles

question: What is the mark of the beast?

scripture_says.text: He causes all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on their right hand or on their foreheads, and that no one may buy or sell except one who has the mark or the name of the beast, or the number of his name
Here is wisdom. Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man: His number is 666
If anyone worships the beast and his image, and receives his mark on his forehead or on his hand, he himself shall also drink of the wine of the wrath of God

history_records.text: 666 has been interpreted using gematria (numerical value of letters) to identify various figures
Some manuscripts read 616 instead of 666

question: What is the new heaven and new earth?

scripture_says.text: I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away. Also there was no more sea
Nevertheless we, according to His promise, look for new heavens and a new earth in which righteousness dwells
Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; and the former shall not be remembered or come to mind
Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and He will dwell with them... God will wipe away every tear from their eyes; there shall be no more death, nor sorrow, nor crying

question: What is the relationship between Israel and the church?

scripture_says.text: Has God cast away His people? Certainly not! For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin
Blindness in part has happened to Israel until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in. And so all Israel will be saved
There is neither Jew nor Greek... for you are all one in Christ Jesus. And if you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise
He Himself is our peace, who has made both one, and has broken down the middle wall of separation... to create in Himself one new man from the two

history_records.text: Major views include replacement theology, dispensationalism, and various mediating positions

question: Can God create a rock He cannot lift?

scripture_says.text: With God nothing will be impossible
Ah, Lord GOD! Behold, You have made the heavens and the earth by Your great power and outstretched arm. There is nothing too hard for You
With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible
God, who cannot lie

history_records.text: This question is known as the omnipotence paradox, discussed by medieval philosophers
Thomas Aquinas argued God cannot do the logically impossible, as contradictions are not 'things'

question: Why are there different Christian denominations?

scripture_says.text: I plead with you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you... Is Christ divided?
That they all may be one, as You, Father, are in Me, and I in You; that they also may be one in Us, that the world may believe that You sent Me
When one says, 'I am of Paul,' and another, 'I am of Apollos,' are you not carnal?

history_records.text: The Great Schism (1054) split Eastern Orthodox and Roman Catholic churches
The Protestant Reformation (16th century) led to numerous Protestant denominations
Denominations often differ on secondary issues while agreeing on core doctrines

scripture_says.text: Whom He foreknew, He also predestined to be conformed to the image of His Son... Moreover whom He predestined, these He also called; whom He called, these He also justified; and whom He justified, these He also glorified
He chose us in Him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before Him in love, having predestined us to adoption as sons by Jesus Christ to Himself
The children not yet being born, nor having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand... 'Jacob I have loved, but Esau I have hated'
The Lord is... not willing that any should perish but that all should come to repentance

history_records.text: Augustine taught predestination based on God's foreknowledge and grace
Calvin emphasized unconditional election; Arminius emphasized conditional election based on foreseen faith

scripture_says.text: I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; neither shall anyone snatch them out of My hand
Neither death nor life... nor any other created thing, shall be able to separate us from the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord
It is impossible for those who were once enlightened... if they fall away, to renew them again to repentance

If, after they have escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they are again entangled in them and overcome, the latter end is worse for them than the beginning

history_records.text: Calvinists teach 'perseverance of the saints' - true believers will persevere to the end
Arminians teach believers can forfeit salvation through unbelief or apostasy

scripture_says.text: Repent, and let every one of you be baptized... For the promise is to you and to your children, and to all who are afar off
He took them the same hour of the night and washed their stripes. And immediately he and all his family were baptized
He who believes and is baptized will be saved
In Him you were also circumcised with the circumcision made without hands... buried with Him in baptism

history_records.text: Evidence of infant baptism dates to at least the 2nd-3rd centuries
Baptists and other groups practice believer's baptism only

question: What is the bread and wine in communion?

scripture_says.text: Jesus took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, 'Take, eat; this is My body.' Then He took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, 'Drink from it, all of you. For this is My blood of the new covenant'
This is My body which is broken for you; do this in remembrance of Me... This cup is the new covenant in My blood. This do, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me
Unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood, you have no life in you... For My flesh is food indeed, and My blood is drink indeed

history_records.text: Roman Catholics teach transubstantiation - bread and wine become Christ's body and blood
Lutherans teach real presence - Christ present 'in, with, and under' the elements
Reformed tradition teaches spiritual presence; memorialists teach symbolic remembrance

scripture_says.text: If anyone's work is burned, he will suffer loss; but he himself will be saved, yet so as through fire
Assuredly, I say to you, you will by no means get out of there till you have paid the last penny

It is appointed for men to die once, but after this the judgment

history_records.text: Purgatory is a doctrine held by the Roman Catholic Church
2 Maccabees 12:39-46 (deuterocanonical) describes prayers for the dead
Protestant reformers rejected purgatory as unbiblical

scripture_says.text: There is one God and one Mediator between God and men, the Man Christ Jesus
Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need
We are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses
The twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb, each having a harp, and golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints

history_records.text: Intercession of saints developed in early Christianity and is practiced by Catholics and Orthodox
Protestants generally pray only to God through Christ

scripture_says.text: The angel came in to her and said, 'Rejoice, highly favored one, the Lord is with you; blessed are you among women!'
My soul magnifies the Lord... For He has regarded the lowly state of His maidservant; for behold, henceforth all generations will call me blessed
His mother said to the servants, 'Whatever He says to you, do it'
Jesus... said to His mother, 'Woman, behold your son!' Then He said to the disciple, 'Behold your mother!'

history_records.text: Mary was called Theotokos ('God-bearer') at the Council of Ephesus (431 AD)
Catholic and Orthodox churches teach special veneration of Mary
Protestants honor Mary as Jesus' mother but do not pray to her

scripture_says.text: All Scripture is given by inspiration of God
All things must be fulfilled which were written in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms concerning Me

history_records.text: The Apocrypha are books included in Catholic and Orthodox Bibles but not in Protestant or Jewish Bibles
These include Tobit, Judith, 1-2 Maccabees, Wisdom, Sirach, Baruch, and additions to Daniel and Esther
Jerome (4th century) distinguished between canonical and 'ecclesiastical' books
The Council of Trent (1546) affirmed these as deuterocanonical

scripture_says.text: God, who at various times and in various ways spoke in time past to the fathers by the prophets, has in these last days spoken to us by His Son
When He, the Spirit of truth, has come, He will guide you into all truth

If anyone adds to these things, God will add to him the plagues that are written in this book

history_records.text: Cessationists believe revelatory gifts ceased with the apostles
Continuationists believe God still speaks through prophecy, dreams, and impressions

scripture_says.text: Take heed that you do not despise one of these little ones, for I say to you that in heaven their angels always see the face of My Father who is in heaven
Are they not all ministering spirits sent forth to minister for those who will inherit salvation?

history_records.text: The concept of guardian angels appears in Jewish tradition and early church writings
